description: Code of practice for information security controls based on ISO/IEC 27002 for cloud services

controls.title: Policies for information security
controls.cloud_guidance: Cloud service providers should define and publish a set of policies specific to cloud computing information security, including how security responsibilities are shared between the CSP and the cloud service customer (CSC).

controls.title: Review of the policies for information security
controls.cloud_guidance: Review should include any changes to the cloud computing environment, including new service offerings, changes in threat landscape, or changes in applicable regulations.

controls.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
controls.cloud_guidance: Both CSP and CSC should define and allocate information security roles and responsibilities related to cloud services. The CSP should clearly document which security functions are the responsibility of the CSC.

controls.title: Contact with authorities
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should identify the relevant authorities in the jurisdictions where cloud data is stored or processed and communicate these to the CSC upon request.

controls.title: Shared roles and responsibilities within a cloud computing environment
controls.cloud_guidance: The allocation of information security roles and responsibilities between the CSP and the CSC should be clearly defined and documented. This should include responsibilities for security controls at the infrastructure, platform, and application layers, and should be communicated through service agreements.

controls.title: Information security awareness, education and training
controls.cloud_guidance: Cloud service provider personnel should receive training specific to cloud security risks, multi-tenancy isolation, data residency requirements, and incident response procedures relevant to cloud environments.

controls.title: Inventory of assets
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSC should maintain an inventory of information and assets stored in the cloud service. The CSP should provide the CSC with the capability to manage their asset inventory.

controls.title: Acceptable use of assets
controls.cloud_guidance: Acceptable use policies should address use of cloud services, including storage limits, permitted data types, and geographic restrictions on data processing.

controls.title: Removal of cloud service customer assets
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide a process for the CSC to retrieve or delete their data and assets upon termination of the cloud service agreement. The CSP should specify the timeframe for which data will be retained post-termination, and ensure that assets are securely removed or returned.

controls.title: Labelling of information
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide mechanisms for the CSC to apply, manage, and identify labels or tags on resources and data within the cloud environment.

controls.title: Access to networks and network services
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should ensure that administrative access to cloud infrastructure is restricted and that network access controls are enforced for cloud service customers. Virtual network segmentation should be implemented.

controls.title: User registration and de-registration
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide the CSC with the capability to manage the registration and de-registration of users within the cloud service. Orphaned accounts should be detectable.

controls.title: User access provisioning
controls.cloud_guidance: Access provisioning to cloud services should be controlled. The CSP should offer role-based access control and the ability for the CSC to define and manage access policies.

controls.title: Management of secret authentication information of users
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide secure mechanisms for managing authentication credentials within the cloud service, including support for multi-factor authentication and credential rotation.

controls.title: Information access restriction
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide access control mechanisms that enable the CSC to restrict access to their information and functions within the cloud environment, including API-level access controls.

controls.title: Use of privileged utility programs
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should restrict and monitor use of privileged utility programs that could override system and application controls in the cloud environment.

controls.title: Segregation in virtual computing environments
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should ensure that a CSC's virtual environment is appropriately segregated from other CSCs and the CSP's own management environment. This includes compute, storage, memory, and network segregation in multi-tenant environments.

controls.title: Virtual machine hardening
controls.cloud_guidance: Virtual machines in the cloud should be hardened to meet security requirements. This includes removing unnecessary services, applying security patches, configuring host-based firewalls, and using approved VM images.

controls.title: Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
controls.cloud_guidance: Both CSP and CSC should define their approach to cryptographic controls for cloud services, including who manages encryption keys. Data should be encrypted in transit and at rest.

controls.title: Key management
controls.cloud_guidance: Key management responsibilities should be clearly divided between the CSP and CSC. The CSP should offer key management services and allow CSCs to manage their own keys where feasible. Hardware security modules (HSMs) should be considered for high-value keys.

controls.title: Physical security perimeter
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should implement physical security perimeters around data center facilities and communicate relevant information about physical security measures to the CSC.

controls.title: Physical entry controls
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should restrict physical access to cloud infrastructure facilities and maintain audit logs of physical access. Access logs should be available to the CSC upon request where applicable.

controls.title: Secure disposal or reuse of equipment
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should ensure that equipment containing cloud service customer data is securely wiped or destroyed before disposal or reuse. The CSP should document the data sanitization procedures used.

controls.title: Change management
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should communicate to the CSC any changes to cloud services that may affect the CSC's information security. Change management procedures should account for multi-tenancy impacts.

controls.title: Capacity management
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should monitor capacity and provide information to the CSC about capacity limits and scaling capabilities. Auto-scaling policies should be configurable by the CSC.

controls.title: Information backup
controls.cloud_guidance: Backup responsibilities should be clearly defined between the CSP and CSC. The CSP should provide backup capabilities and the CSC should verify that backup and restoration procedures are adequate for their requirements.

controls.title: Event logging
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide logging capabilities for the CSC and maintain logs of its own administrative actions. Log data should be available to the CSC in a standard format, and log integrity should be protected.

controls.title: Administrator and operator logs
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should log all administrative and operator activities on the cloud infrastructure. These logs should be protected from tampering and made available for audit purposes.

controls.title: Clock synchronisation
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should use synchronised time sources across all cloud infrastructure to ensure consistent timestamps for logging and audit purposes.

controls.title: Administrator's operational security
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should implement procedures to ensure that cloud administrators follow operational security practices, including secure access methods, segregation of administrative duties, and monitoring of administrative sessions.

controls.title: Monitoring of cloud services
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSC should have the capability to monitor specific aspects of the cloud service operation. The CSP should provide monitoring tools, health dashboards, and alerting capabilities to enable the CSC to verify the operational status and security of their cloud resources.

controls.title: Segregation in networks
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should implement network segregation between cloud service customers. Virtual networks, security groups, and network access control lists should isolate customer traffic.

controls.title: Alignment of security management for virtual and physical networks
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should define and apply a consistent information security policy for both virtual and physical networks. Virtual network controls should provide equivalent security to physical network controls, including monitoring, access control, and intrusion detection.

controls.title: Information security requirements analysis and specification
controls.cloud_guidance: Security requirements for cloud services should address multi-tenancy, data isolation, secure APIs, data residency, portability, and interoperability requirements.

controls.title: Secure development policy
controls.cloud_guidance: Cloud services should be developed using secure development practices, including secure API design, input validation, and protection against common cloud-specific vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Information security policy for supplier relationships
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide information about its supply chain to the CSC, including any sub-processors or third-party services used to deliver the cloud service.

controls.title: Addressing security within supplier agreements
controls.cloud_guidance: Cloud service agreements should clearly address information security requirements, including data handling, incident notification, audit rights, and exit procedures.

controls.title: Information and communication technology supply chain
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should manage ICT supply chain risks for components used in the cloud service and provide transparency about the supply chain to the CSC.

controls.title: Responsibilities and procedures
controls.cloud_guidance: Incident management responsibilities should be clearly defined between the CSP and CSC. The CSP should have procedures for detecting, reporting, and responding to incidents affecting cloud service customers.

controls.title: Reporting information security events
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide mechanisms for reporting security events and define notification timelines. The CSC should be notified of security events that affect their data or services.

controls.title: Collection of evidence
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should support the CSC in collecting digital evidence related to security incidents. The CSP should document its evidence collection and preservation procedures, including chain of custody across jurisdictions.

sections.name: Information security aspects of business continuity management

controls.title: Planning information security continuity
controls.cloud_guidance: Business continuity planning should address cloud service dependencies. The CSC should consider the CSP's continuity capabilities, data replication options, and failover mechanisms.

controls.title: Implementing information security continuity
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should provide the CSC with information about its continuity and disaster recovery capabilities, including recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO).

controls.title: Availability of information processing facilities
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should implement redundancy to meet agreed availability requirements. This includes multi-zone and multi-region deployment options, and the CSP should communicate availability SLAs to the CSC.

controls.title: Identification of applicable legislation and contractual requirements
controls.cloud_guidance: Both CSP and CSC should identify applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements for cloud services. The CSP should communicate the jurisdictions in which data may be stored or processed.

controls.title: Protection of records
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should support the CSC in meeting record retention requirements and ensure that records stored in the cloud are protected from loss, destruction, and falsification.

controls.title: Regulation of cryptographic controls
controls.cloud_guidance: Both CSP and CSC should consider the regulatory implications of cryptographic controls in the jurisdictions where cloud services operate, including restrictions on import/export of cryptographic technology.

controls.title: Independent review of information security
controls.cloud_guidance: The CSP should conduct regular independent reviews of its cloud service security and make results available to the CSC. Third-party audit reports (e.g. SOC 2, ISO 27001 certification) should be provided upon request.
